chemistry.reaction_name: Chloride-Mediated Peroxide-Free Photochemical Oxidation of Proteins (PPOP)

photocatalyst.name: Chloride ions (Cl⁻)
photocatalyst.role: Photoexcitable species that generates chlorine radicals

chemistry.product: Oxidized protein residues with +15.99 Da (mono-oxidation) and +31.99 Da (di-oxidation) modifications
chemistry.bond_formed: C-O bonds in amino acid side chains
chemistry.scope_summary: Method applicable to various proteins including BSA, HSA, myoglobin, and cytochrome c. Oxidizes amino acid residues in order of reactivity: Cys > Trp > Tyr > Met > Phe > His > Arg > Leu > Ile. Can probe protein-drug binding interfaces and structural changes.

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: focused to about 2 mm width through a 250 mm convex lens

figures.key_information: Single-pulsed capillary flow reactor with 193 nm laser irradiation for protein hydroxylation without adding H2O2, comparing to prior work using 249/266 nm laser with H2O2/PBS solution
193 nm laser irradiation of flowing solution in capillary for protein hydroxylation without H2O2 addition, showing chloride ion excitation and interaction with proteins
figures.caption: Figure 1. (a) Prior works rely on 248/266 nm photolysis of H2O2 to produce •OH for protein oxidation. (b) This work uses chloride-mediated peroxide-free photochemical oxidation of proteins (PPOP) under direct 193 nm photoirradiation of PBS solution. (c) Capillary flow photoreactor for PPOP.

Figure 2. (a) Standard peptides with or without single-pulse of 193 nm laser irradiation. (b) Two randomly selected BSA tryptic peptides Asp319-Lys340 and Arg508-Lys523 with or without 193 nm laser irradiation. (c) Photochemical oxidation of BSA tryptic peptides with different concentrations of Cl− (not containing phosphate ions). (d) +18O photochemical modification fractions of representat

Figure 3. (a, b) Photochemical oxidation peptides and sites of intact myoglobin (a) and BSA (b) with relatively high peptide modification fraction (>30%) within the PBS solution. (c) Comparison of oxidation regions and modification fractions between intact and digested forms of BSA. (d) Regions with significant modification fraction differences between intact and digested forms of BSA are sh

Figure 4. (a) Comparison of the HSA oxidation regions and modification fraction with or without warfarin combination. (b) Peptide regions with significant differences in photochemical oxidation upon warfarin combination, and (c) their locations in HSA structure (PDB: 2bxd, shown as a smudge-green cartoon).

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Enables controlled exposure to laser irradiation and continuous processing of protein samples
translation_logic.what_changed: Developed peroxide-free method using chloride-mediated hydroxyl radical generation with 193 nm laser instead of traditional H2O2-based methods
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Protein footprinting approach using hydroxyl radical oxidation for structural analysis
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Eliminates need for hydrogen peroxide addition

figure_descriptions: Capillary flow reactor scheme with laser irradiation setup
Comparison between traditional H2O2-based and new chloride-mediated protein oxidation methods

notes: This is a novel peroxide-free protein footprinting method using flow chemistry. No traditional batch baseline comparison provided. Focus is on analytical method development rather than synthetic optimization.
